text: Reduction of a nitrile (R–CN) with LiAlH₄ gives:
explanation: R–CN + LiAlH₄ → R–CH₂–NH₂ (primary amine). The nitrile carbon is reduced to –CH₂–. Useful synthetic route.
name: Reduction of Nitrile
subject: che

text: The displacement in the \(n\)th second of uniformly accelerated motion is:
explanation: \( s_n = u + \frac{a}{2}(2n-1) \). This is displacement in the nth second specifically.
name: Displacement in nth Second
subject: phy

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The critical points of \( f(x) \) are points where:
explanation: Critical points: where \( f'(x) = 0 \) or \( f'(x) \) does not exist. Extrema can only occur at critical points.
name: Critical Points
subject: maths

text: Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity states that electrons occupy degenerate orbitals:
options: One each with same spin before pairing
explanation: Hund's Rule: Electrons occupy orbitals of equal energy singly with same spin before pairing begins.
name: Hund's Rule
subject: che

text: The equation of tangent to \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) at point \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Tangent at \((x_1,y_1)\): \( xx_1+yy_1=r^2 \). Replace \(x^2\to xx_1\) and \(y^2\to yy_1\).
name: Tangent at Point
subject: maths

text: If \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{pmatrix} \), then \( |A| = \)
name: 2x2 Determinant
subject: maths

text: The product formula \( 2\sin A \cos B = \)
explanation: Product-to-Sum: \( 2\sin A\cos B = \sin(A+B) + \sin(A-B) \)
name: Product-to-Sum
subject: maths

text: The Quotient Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\left(\frac{u}{v}\right) = \)
explanation: Quotient Rule: \( \left(\frac{u}{v}\right)' = \frac{u'v - uv'}{v^2} \)
name: Quotient Rule
subject: maths

text: The time period of a bar magnet oscillating in a uniform magnetic field \(B\) is:
explanation: Magnet in field: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{I}{MB}} \), where \(I\) = moment of inertia, \(M\) = magnetic moment, \(B\) = field.
name: Magnet Oscillation Period
subject: phy

text: At resonance in an LCR series circuit:
options: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow Z = R \) (minimum impedance, maximum current)
explanation: Resonance: \( X_L = X_C \Rightarrow \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Impedance = \(R\) (minimum). Current = \(V/R\) (maximum). Power factor = 1.
name: LCR Resonance
subject: phy

text: The slope of line joining \( (2,3) \) and \( (5,9) \) is:
name: Slope of Line
subject: maths

text: At a local maximum, the second derivative satisfies:
explanation: Second Derivative Test: local max iff \( f'(x)=0 \) and \( f''(x)<0 \).
name: Second Derivative Test - Max
subject: maths

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \sqrt{x - 3} \) is:
explanation: For \(\sqrt{x-3}\) to be defined, \( x - 3 \geq 0 \Rightarrow x \geq 3 \)
name: Domain of Square Root
subject: maths

text: Biodegradable polymers are preferred because they:
options: Can be broken down by microorganisms, reducing pollution
explanation: Biodegradable polymers (e.g., PHBV, PLA, nylon-2-nylon-6): degraded by microorganisms into CO₂, H₂O, and biomass. Reduce plastic pollution.
name: Biodegradable Polymers
subject: che

text: An object is placed at 30 cm from a convex lens of focal length 10 cm. The image distance is:
explanation: \( \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{-30} = \frac{1}{10} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{v} = \frac{1}{10}-\frac{1}{30} = \frac{3-1}{30} = \frac{2}{30} \Rightarrow v = 15\,\text{cm} \).
name: Lens Image Distance Calculation
subject: phy

text: The integrating factor for \( \frac{dy}{dx} + Py = Q \) is:
explanation: For linear DE \( y'+Py=Q \), integrating factor (IF) \( = e^{\int P\,dx} \).
name: Integrating Factor
subject: maths

text: If \( A \) is a \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix, then \( |kA| = \)
explanation: For an \(n \times n\) matrix: \( |kA| = k^n|A| \). Here \(n=3\), so \(|kA| = k^3|A|\).
name: Scalar Multiple Determinant
subject: maths

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The scalar triple product \( [\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}] = \vec{a}\cdot(\vec{b}\times\vec{c}) \) gives:
explanation: \( |[\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}]| \) = volume of parallelepiped formed by \(\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}\).
name: Scalar Triple Product
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6 = 0 \), then \( \alpha + \beta + \gamma = \)
explanation: For cubic \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \), sum of roots \( = -p = -(-6) = 6 \)
name: Sum of Roots of Cubic
subject: maths

text: A galvanometer is converted to a voltmeter by connecting:
explanation: Voltmeter: high resistance \(R\) in series with galvanometer. \( V = I_g(G + R) \Rightarrow R = V/I_g - G \). High resistance so very little current drawn.
name: Galvanometer to Voltmeter
subject: phy

text: Van de Graaff generator is based on:
options: Electrostatic induction and the fact that charge distributes on outer surface of conductor
explanation: Van de Graaff generator: charge sprayed on belt, carried to hollow sphere where it distributes on outer surface. Can build up very high voltages (MV range).
name: Van de Graaff Generator Principle
subject: phy

text: The number of diagonals of a polygon with \(n\) sides is:
explanation: Total lines \( = \binom{n}{2} \), minus \(n\) sides: diagonals \( = \frac{n(n-1)}{2} - n = \frac{n(n-3)}{2} \)
name: Diagonals of Polygon
subject: maths

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{f(x)}{(x-a)^2(x-b)} \) is:
explanation: For repeated linear factor \((x-a)^2\), decompose as \( \frac{A}{x-a} + \frac{B}{(x-a)^2} \), plus \( \frac{C}{x-b} \).
name: Repeated Linear Factors
subject: maths

text: Melde's experiment demonstrates the formation of standing waves on a string. The number of loops \(p\) is related to tension \(T\) by:
explanation: Melde's experiment: \( p = \frac{L}{\lambda/2} \), frequency \( f = p\frac{v}{2L} = p\frac{1}{2L}\sqrt{T/\mu} \). So \( p \propto 1/\sqrt{T} \) at fixed \(f\).
name: Melde's Experiment
subject: phy

text: \( \coth^2 x - \text{csch}^2 x = \)
explanation: Dividing \( \cosh^2x - \sinh^2x = 1 \) by \( \sinh^2x \): \( \coth^2x - \text{csch}^2x = 1 \)
name: coth² - csch² Identity
subject: maths

text: The permittivity of free space \(\epsilon_0\) has value:
explanation: \( \epsilon_0 = 8.85\times10^{-12}\,\text{C}^2/(\text{N m}^2) \). Note: \( k = 1/4\pi\epsilon_0 \approx 9\times10^9\,\text{N m}^2/\text{C}^2 \).
name: Permittivity of Free Space
subject: phy

text: If \( A \) is a symmetric matrix, then \( A^T = \)
explanation: A matrix is symmetric if \( A^T = A \) (transpose equals itself).
name: Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: In SHM, the displacement equation is \( x = A\sin(\omega t + \phi) \). The velocity is:
explanation: \( v = \frac{dx}{dt} = A\omega\cos(\omega t + \phi) \). Maximum velocity \( v_{max} = A\omega \) (at equilibrium position).
name: Velocity in SHM
subject: phy

text: A perfect blackbody has emissivity:
explanation: Perfect blackbody: \(\epsilon = 1\) (absorbs/emits radiation perfectly). Real bodies: \(0 < \epsilon < 1\). Good absorbers are good emitters (Kirchhoff's law).
name: Blackbody Emissivity
subject: phy

text: The greatest binomial coefficient in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
explanation: For even \(n = 10\), greatest coefficient is the middle one: \( \binom{10}{5} = 252 \)
name: Greatest Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: Relative lowering of vapour pressure is equal to:
explanation: \( \frac{P^\circ - P}{P^\circ} = x_{\text{solute}} \). Relative lowering = mole fraction of solute.
name: Relative Lowering of VP
subject: che

text: According to MO theory, which molecule is paramagnetic?
explanation: \(\text{O}_2\) MO configuration has 2 unpaired electrons in \(\pi^*\) orbitals → paramagnetic. This was a triumph of MO theory.
name: Paramagnetic Molecule
subject: che

text: The speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum is:
explanation: Speed of light: \( c = 1/\sqrt{\mu_0\epsilon_0} \approx 3\times10^8\,\text{m/s} \). Maxwell derived this and identified light as EM wave.
name: Speed of EM Waves
subject: phy

text: The equation of a line with slope \(m\) and y-intercept \(c\) is:
explanation: Slope-intercept form: \( y = mx + c \)
name: Slope-Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The volume occupied by 1 mole of an ideal gas at STP (0°C, 1 atm) is:
explanation: Molar volume of ideal gas at STP = 22.4 L/mol (standard condition: 0°C, 1 atm).
name: Molar Volume at STP
subject: che

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2) \) is:
explanation: Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2 + (y_2-y_1)^2} \)
name: Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: The electric field on the axial line of a dipole at large distance \(r\) is:
explanation: Axial field: \( E_{axial} = \frac{2p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \) (along dipole axis). Equatorial: \( E_{eq} = \frac{p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \). Ratio = 2:1.
name: Dipole Axial Field
subject: phy

text: The acidity of carboxylic acids compared to alcohols is due to:
explanation: RCOOH (pKa ~5) >> ROH (pKa ~16). The carboxylate anion –COO⁻ is resonance-stabilised (negative charge delocalised over 2 oxygens).
name: Acidity of Carboxylic Acids
subject: che

text: The equation \( 2\cos^2\theta - \cos\theta - 1 = 0 \) in \([0, 2\pi]\) — the number of solutions is:
explanation: Factor: \( (2\cos\theta+1)(\cos\theta-1) = 0 \Rightarrow \cos\theta = -\frac{1}{2} \) (gives \(\frac{2\pi}{3}, \frac{4\pi}{3}\)) or \(\cos\theta=1\) (gives \(0\)). Total: 3 solutions.
name: Quadratic in cosθ
subject: maths

text: KMnO₄ in acidic medium acts as:
explanation: In acidic medium: \( \text{MnO}_4^- + 8\text{H}^+ + 5e^- \to \text{Mn}^{2+} + 4\text{H}_2\text{O} \). KMnO₄ is a powerful oxidant. Mn: +7 → +2.
name: KMnO₄ in Acidic Medium
subject: che

text: Bond dissociation enthalpy is used to calculate \( \Delta H_{rxn} \) as:
explanation: \( \Delta H = \sum BE_{\text{bonds broken}} - \sum BE_{\text{bonds formed}} \). Breaking bonds absorbs energy; forming bonds releases it.
name: ΔH from Bond Energies
subject: che

text: The activity \(A\) of a radioactive sample is:
explanation: Activity: \( A = \lambda N = \lambda N_0 e^{-\lambda t} = A_0 e^{-\lambda t} \). Unit: Becquerel (Bq) = 1 decay/s. 1 Curie = 3.7×10¹⁰ Bq.
name: Radioactive Activity
subject: phy

text: The range of \( f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 5 \) is:
explanation: Minimum at \( x=2 \): \( f(2) = 4-8+5 = 1 \). Since \(a>0\), range is \([1, \infty)\)
name: Range of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: The Compton effect involves:
options: Scattering of X-ray photon by free electron (wavelength shift: \( \Delta\lambda = \frac{h}{m_ec}(1-\cos\phi) \))
explanation: Compton scattering: X-ray photon scattered by free electron. Wavelength increases: \( \Delta\lambda = \frac{h}{m_ec}(1-\cos\phi) \). Compton wavelength \( = h/m_ec = 0.0243\) Å.
name: Compton Effect
subject: phy

text: The total number of spectral lines possible from the nth excited state is:
explanation: Number of lines from level \(n\): \( ^nC_2 = n(n-1)/2 \). From n=4: \( 4\times3/2 = 6 \) lines possible.
name: Number of Spectral Lines
subject: phy

text: The EMF of a cell is given by:
explanation: \( E_{cell} = E_{cathode} - E_{anode} \) (reduction potentials). For spontaneous cell: \(E_{cell} > 0\).
name: EMF of Cell
subject: che

text: Chloroform (CHCl₃) when oxidised in air slowly gives:
explanation: CHCl₃ oxidises in air to form phosgene (COCl₂), which is highly toxic. That's why CHCl₃ is stored in dark bottles with a little ethanol.
name: Oxidation of Chloroform
subject: che

text: Walli's formula: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^m x\cos^n x\,dx \) involves the pattern of multiplying down by \(2\) at the end when both \(m\) and \(n\) are:
explanation: When both \(m\) and \(n\) are even, Walli's formula requires an additional factor of \(\frac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Walli's Formula
subject: maths

text: The centroid of tetrahedron with vertices \( A,B,C,D \) is:
explanation: Centroid of tetrahedron = average of all 4 vertices' coordinates.
name: Centroid of Tetrahedron
subject: maths

text: The Williamson synthesis of ethers involves:
options: Reaction of sodium alkoxide with alkyl halide (SN2)
explanation: Williamson synthesis: R–O⁻Na⁺ + R'–X → R–O–R' + NaX. Best route for unsymmetrical ethers. SN2 mechanism.
name: Williamson Synthesis
subject: che

text: The incenter of triangle with vertices \( A, B, C \) and opposite sides \( a, b, c \) is:
explanation: Incenter \( = \left(\frac{ax_A+bx_B+cx_C}{a+b+c}, \frac{ay_A+by_B+cy_C}{a+b+c}\right) \) (weighted by opposite side lengths).
name: Incenter Formula
subject: maths

text: The standard enthalpy of formation (\( \Delta H^\circ_f \)) of an element in its standard state is:
explanation: By definition, \( \Delta H^\circ_f = 0 \) for any element in its standard state (e.g., O₂(g), C(graphite)).
name: Standard Enthalpy of Element
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) \) is an odd function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Odd function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 0 \) (symmetry about origin cancels)
name: Odd Function Integral
subject: maths

text: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky (HVZ) reaction introduces halogen at _____ position of carboxylic acids:
explanation: HVZ reaction: RCOOH + X₂ (PCl₃/PBr₃ catalyst) → α-halocarboxylic acid. X introduced at α-carbon of the acid.
name: HVZ Reaction
subject: che

text: The law of definite proportions (Proust's Law) states:
options: Gases combine in simple whole-number volumes
A compound always has elements in fixed mass ratio
Equal volumes of gases have equal molecules
explanation: Proust's Law: A pure compound always contains elements combined in a fixed definite proportion by mass.
name: Law of Definite Proportions
subject: che

text: The Sandwich theorem (Squeeze theorem) states: if \( g(x)\leq f(x)\leq h(x) \) and \( \lim g = \lim h = L \), then:
explanation: Squeeze Theorem: if \(f\) is squeezed between \(g\) and \(h\), and both converge to \(L\), then \(\lim f = L\).
name: Squeeze Theorem
subject: maths

text: Optical isomerism (enantiomers) is possible in:
options: Octahedral complexes with three bidentate ligands [M(AA)₃]
explanation: Optical isomers (non-superimposable mirror images) occur in [M(AA)₃] type octahedral complexes (e.g., [Co(en)₃]³⁺). Also [M(AA)₂BC]²⁺.
name: Optical Isomerism in Complexes
subject: che

text: For a solution showing positive deviation from Raoult's law:
explanation: Positive deviation: \(P > P_{Raoult}\), \(\Delta H_{mix} > 0\) (endothermic), \(\Delta V_{mix} > 0\). Example: ethanol + water.
name: Positive Deviation
subject: che

text: Fehling's solution is reduced by:
explanation: Fehling's test: gives brick-red Cu₂O precipitate with aliphatic aldehydes. Aromatic aldehydes (benzaldehyde) do NOT reduce Fehling's. Ketones: negative.
name: Fehling's Test
subject: che

text: In \( (2x - 3y)^5 \), the sum of coefficients (setting \(x=y=1\)) is:
name: Sum of Coefficients
subject: maths

text: The correct order of first ionisation energies of Group 1 elements is:
explanation: IE decreases down Group 1 (larger atoms, easier to lose electrons): Li(520) > Na(496) > K(419) > Rb(403) > Cs(376) kJ/mol.
name: Group 1 Ionisation Energy
subject: che

text: Classical (London) smog is primarily composed of:
options: SO₂ + particulates (soot/fog) in cold, damp conditions
explanation: Classical smog (London type): SO₂ + particulates/smoke + fog. Formed in cool, damp, stagnant air. Opposite: photochemical smog (warm, sunny).
name: Classical Smog
subject: che

text: Lucas test distinguishes between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols using:
explanation: Lucas test: 3° alcohol → immediate turbidity (SN1, stable 3° carbocation). 2° → turbidity in 5 min. 1° → no turbidity at room temperature.
name: Lucas Test
subject: che

text: The stability of chelate complexes compared to analogous non-chelate complexes is known as:
explanation: Chelate effect: chelate complexes are thermodynamically more stable than analogous non-chelate complexes due to increased entropy and ring formation.
name: Chelate Effect
subject: che

text: Which type of solid has the highest melting point?
explanation: Covalent network solids (e.g., diamond, SiO₂) have extremely high melting points due to strong covalent bonds throughout.
name: Highest Melting Point Solid
subject: che

text: In thin film interference, the condition for bright reflected light from film of thickness \(t\) and refractive index \(n\) is (both reflections at denser medium):
explanation: Thin film (same phase change at both surfaces): Constructive: \( 2nt = (2m-1)\lambda/2 \). Destructive: \( 2nt = m\lambda \). (One phase change at each interface.)
name: Thin Film Interference
subject: phy

text: For an exothermic reaction at equilibrium, increasing temperature:
explanation: For exothermic reaction: heat is a product. Increasing T shifts equilibrium left → less products → K decreases.
name: Temperature Effect on K
subject: che

text: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². Its angular acceleration is:
explanation: \( \alpha = \tau/I = 10/2 = 5\,\text{rad/s}^2 \). Analogous to \( a = F/m \).
name: Angular Acceleration Calculation
subject: phy

text: The speed of a transverse wave on a string with tension \(T\) and linear mass density \(\mu\) is:
explanation: Wave speed on string: \( v = \sqrt{T/\mu} \). Higher tension or lower mass density → faster wave.
name: Wave Speed on String
subject: phy

text: The equation with roots \( \frac{1}{\alpha}, \frac{1}{\beta}, \frac{1}{\gamma} \) (where \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma\) are roots of \( x^3-2x^2+3x-4=0 \)) is:
name: Reciprocal Roots Equation
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to a} \frac{x^n - a^n}{x - a} = \)
explanation: Standard algebraic limit: \( \lim_{x\to a}\frac{x^n-a^n}{x-a} = na^{n-1} \)
name: Algebraic Limit
subject: maths

text: Hydrogen bonding is strongest in:
explanation: H-bonding strength depends on electronegativity and size of atom. F is most electronegative and smallest → strongest H-bond in HF.
name: Strongest Hydrogen Bond
subject: che

text: The standard electrode potential of hydrogen electrode (SHE) is:
explanation: The Standard Hydrogen Electrode (SHE) is the reference electrode with E° = 0.00 V by definition.
name: SHE Potential
subject: che

text: Ohm's law states:
explanation: Ohm's law: \( V = IR \). Valid for ohmic conductors at constant temperature. SI unit of resistance: Ohm (Ω).
name: Ohm's Law
subject: phy

text: In a head-on elastic collision between equal masses where one is at rest, after collision:
options: First stops, second moves with first's velocity
explanation: Equal mass elastic collision: first body stops, second moves with velocity of first. Velocities exchange completely.
name: Elastic Collision Equal Masses
subject: phy

text: The relation between orbital velocity \(v_o\) and escape velocity \(v_e\) is:
explanation: \( v_o = \sqrt{GM/r} \), \( v_e = \sqrt{2GM/r} = \sqrt{2}\,v_o \). Escape velocity is \(\sqrt{2}\) times orbital velocity.
name: Escape vs Orbital Velocity
subject: phy

explanation: At \(x=3\): \( 6-1 = B(4) \Rightarrow B = \frac{5}{4} \)
name: Coefficient B
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = \)
explanation: Definition of \(e\): \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = e \approx 2.718 \)
name: Definition of e
subject: maths

text: Diagonal relationship exists between:
explanation: Diagonal relationship: Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si. Elements show similarity with the element diagonally below-right due to similar charge/size ratio.
name: Diagonal Relationship
subject: che

text: The order of stability of carbocations is:
explanation: Carbocation stability: 3° > 2° > 1° > methyl. More alkyl groups → more +I effect → better stabilisation of positive charge.
name: Carbocation Stability
subject: che

text: Number of ways to arrange 5 books on a shelf is:
explanation: Arrangements of 5 distinct items \( = 5! = 120 \)
name: Arrangements
subject: maths

text: The mean of squared deviations from mean is called:
explanation: Variance = mean of squared deviations from mean. SD is its square root.
name: Variance Definition
subject: maths

text: In an equilateral triangle with side \(a\), the area is:
explanation: For equilateral triangle: \( \Delta = \frac{1}{2}a\cdot a\sin 60° = \frac{a^2\sqrt{3}}{4} \)
name: Equilateral Triangle Area
subject: maths

text: Number of functions from a set of 3 elements to a set of 2 elements is:
explanation: Each of 3 elements can map to 2 choices. Total \( = 2^3 = 8 \)
name: Number of Functions
subject: maths

text: For the reaction \( 2\text{SO}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{SO}_3 \), if \(K_c = 4\) for the forward reaction, then \(K_c\) for the reverse reaction is:
explanation: For reverse reaction: \( K_c' = \frac{1}{K_c} = \frac{1}{4} = 0.25 \).
name: K for Reverse Reaction
subject: che

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: The coefficient of restitution \(e\) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
explanation: \( e = \frac{\text{relative speed of separation}}{\text{relative speed of approach}} \). Perfectly elastic: \(e=1\), Perfectly inelastic: \(e=0\).
name: Coefficient of Restitution
subject: phy

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: For a conical pendulum, the tension in the string provides:
options: Both vertical support and centripetal force (horizontal component provides centripetal)
explanation: Conical pendulum: \( T\cos\theta = mg \) (vertical), \( T\sin\theta = \frac{mv^2}{r} \) (centripetal horizontal).
name: Conical Pendulum
subject: phy

text: Wurtz reaction is used to synthesise:
options: Higher alkanes from alkyl halides + Na
explanation: Wurtz reaction: 2R–X + 2Na → R–R + 2NaX. Joins two alkyl groups. Best for symmetrical alkanes.
name: Wurtz Reaction
subject: che

text: The pair of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: The lines are perpendicular when \( a + b = 0 \) (sum of coefficients of \(x^2\) and \(y^2\) is zero).
name: Perpendicular Pair
subject: maths

text: Resonance in a forced oscillation occurs when:
explanation: Resonance: when driving frequency \( \omega_d = \omega_0 \) (natural frequency). Amplitude becomes maximum. Can cause structural failure.
name: Resonance Condition
subject: phy

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \log(x^2 - x - 6) \) is:
explanation: Need \( x^2-x-6 > 0 \Rightarrow (x-3)(x+2) > 0 \Rightarrow x > 3 \) or \( x < -2 \)
name: Domain of Logarithm
subject: maths

text: The equation of line through \((x_1,y_1)\) with slope \(m\) is:
explanation: Point-slope form: \( y - y_1 = m(x - x_1) \)
name: Point-Slope Form
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \tan 45° = \)
name: Standard Value tan 45
subject: maths

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) to plane \( ax+by+cz+d=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to plane: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+cz_1+d|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2+c^2}} \)
name: Point to Plane Distance
subject: maths

text: The quartile deviation is defined as:
explanation: Quartile Deviation (semi-interquartile range) \( = \frac{Q_3 - Q_1}{2} \)
name: Quartile Deviation
subject: maths

text: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius \(R\) is:
explanation: Soap bubble has 2 surfaces: \( \Delta P = \frac{4T}{R} \). Single liquid drop: \( \Delta P = \frac{2T}{R} \).
name: Excess Pressure in Soap Bubble
subject: phy

text: The value of \( i^{97} \) is:
explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i
subject: maths

text: \( 2^n \geq n^2 \) holds for all \( n \geq \)
explanation: Check: \(n=3\): \(8 < 9\) (fails), \(n=4\): \(16 = 16\) (holds), \(n \geq 4\): true by induction.
name: Exponential vs Polynomial
subject: maths

text: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = \)
explanation: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\sin\theta \). Area of parallelogram = \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Magnitude of Cross Product
subject: maths

text: Anti-Markovnikov addition (peroxide effect) occurs in addition of HBr to alkenes when:
explanation: In presence of peroxides, HBr adds via free radical mechanism (anti-Markovnikov). Br goes to carbon with more H (opposite to ionic). Only HBr shows this.
name: Peroxide Effect
subject: che

text: For ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), eccentricity \( e = \)
explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: Power factor of an AC circuit is defined as:
explanation: Power factor: \( \cos\phi = R/Z \). Average power: \( P = V_{rms}I_{rms}\cos\phi \). For pure R: \(\cos\phi=1\). For pure L or C: \(\cos\phi=0\).
name: Power Factor
subject: phy

explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{a^2+x^2}dx = \frac{1}{a}\tan^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Standard Form Integral
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \binom{2n}{n} \) grows approximately as:
explanation: By Stirling's approximation: \( \binom{2n}{n} \sim \frac{4^n}{\sqrt{\pi n}} \) for large \(n\).
name: Central Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: The f-block elements (lanthanides) have the electron being filled in:
explanation: Lanthanides (Ce to Lu): filling of 4f orbitals. Actinides: filling of 5f orbitals. Both are inner transition elements.
name: f-Block Orbital Filling
subject: che

text: The total probability theorem states \( P(B) = \sum P(A_i) \cdot P(B|A_i) \) when \( A_i \) form:
explanation: The theorem applies when \( A_1, A_2, \ldots \) form a mutually exclusive and exhaustive partition of the sample space.
name: Total Probability Theorem
subject: maths

text: The expression \( x^2 + x + 1 \) is positive for:
explanation: Discriminant \( = 1 - 4 = -3 < 0 \) and \( a = 1 > 0 \), so expression is positive for all real \( x \).
name: Sign of Quadratic Expression
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor
subject: phy

text: The gravitational field intensity at a distance \(r\) outside a uniform sphere of mass \(M\) is:
explanation: Outside sphere: \( g = \frac{GM}{r^2} \) (same as point mass). Inside: \( g = \frac{GM}{R^3}r \propto r \).
name: Gravitational Field
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Hyperbola (transverse axis along x): \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \). Eccentricity \(e > 1\).
name: Hyperbola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The magnifying power of a simple microscope at near point (D=25 cm) is:
explanation: Simple microscope: \( M = 1 + D/f \) (image at near point). \( M = D/f \) (image at infinity). Near point \(D = 25\) cm.
name: Magnifying Power of Simple Microscope
subject: phy

text: The curve \( y = f(x) \) is concave upward when:
explanation: Concave upward (cup shape) when \( f''(x) > 0 \). Concave downward when \( f''(x) < 0 \).
name: Concavity
subject: maths

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The parallel axis theorem states:
explanation: Parallel axis theorem: \( I = I_{cm} + Md^2 \), where \(d\) = distance from CM axis to new parallel axis.
name: Parallel Axis Theorem
subject: phy

text: The units of rate constant \(k\) for a second-order reaction are:
explanation: Units of \(k\): \( [k] = \frac{[\text{rate}]}{[\text{conc}]^n} = \frac{\text{mol L}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1}}{(\text{mol L}^{-1})^2} = \text{L mol}^{-1}\text{s}^{-1} \) for 2nd order.
name: Units of k (2nd Order)
subject: che

text: The number of solutions of \( 2\sin^2\theta = 1 \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) is:
explanation: \( \sin^2\theta = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \sin\theta = \pm\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{3\pi}{4}, \frac{5\pi}{4}, \frac{7\pi}{4} \). That's 4 solutions.
name: Number of Solutions
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: \( \int_0^\pi \sin x\,dx = \)
name: Definite Integral of sin
subject: maths

text: A body of mass 2 kg moving at 3 m/s has kinetic energy:
name: KE Calculation
subject: phy

text: Brewster's angle (polarising angle) \(\theta_B\) is related to refractive index by:
explanation: Brewster's law: \( \tan\theta_B = n \) (\(n\) = refractive index of second medium). At this angle, reflected light is completely plane-polarised.
name: Brewster's Law
subject: phy

text: If \( z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta) \), then \( z^n \) by De-Moivre's theorem equals:
explanation: De-Moivre's Theorem: \( z^n = r^n(\cos n\theta + i\sin n\theta) \)
name: De-Moivre's Theorem
subject: maths

text: The slope of line \( 3x - 4y + 7 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Rewrite: \( y = \frac{3}{4}x + \frac{7}{4} \). Slope \( m = \frac{3}{4} \).
name: Slope from General Equation
subject: maths

text: Resonance structures of \( \text{O}_3 \) show that the actual O–O bond order is:
explanation: Ozone has two resonance structures (one O=O and one O–O). Average bond order = (1+2)/2 = 1.5.
name: Resonance in Ozone
subject: che

text: A skew-symmetric matrix satisfies:
explanation: A matrix is skew-symmetric if \( A^T = -A \), i.e., \( A + A^T = 0 \).
name: Skew-Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: The mass action law for semiconductors states:
options: \( n_e \cdot n_h = n_i^2 \) (constant at given temperature)
explanation: Mass action law: \( n_e n_h = n_i^2 \). Doping increases one carrier type but decreases the other. Product remains constant at given T.
name: Mass Action Law
subject: phy

text: When capacitors are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance is:
explanation: Parallel: \( C_{eq} = C_1 + C_2 + \cdots \) (capacitances add). Series: \( 1/C_{eq} = 1/C_1 + 1/C_2 + \cdots \).
name: Capacitors in Parallel
subject: phy

text: The energy of an electron in the nth orbit of hydrogen atom is given by:
explanation: Bohr's model: \( E_n = -\frac{13.6}{n^2} \) eV. Ground state (n=1): \(-13.6\) eV.
name: Bohr's Energy Formula
subject: che

text: The equation of circle with diameter endpoints \((x_1,y_1)\) and \((x_2,y_2)\) is:
explanation: Diameter form: \( (x-x_1)(x-x_2)+(y-y_1)(y-y_2)=0 \) (angle in semicircle = 90°).
name: Circle with Diameter
subject: maths

text: The term independent of \(x\) in \( \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^6 \) is:
name: Term Independent of x
subject: maths

text: Area of triangle \(ABC\) using two sides and included angle is:
explanation: Area \( = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C = \frac{1}{2}bc\sin A = \frac{1}{2}ca\sin B \)
name: Area of Triangle
subject: maths

text: A heat engine absorbs \(Q_H = 1000\,\text{J}\) and rejects \(Q_C = 600\,\text{J}\). Its efficiency is:
name: Heat Engine Efficiency Calculation
subject: phy

text: The hybridisation of carbon in benzene is:
explanation: Benzene: each C is sp² hybridised, forming 3 σ bonds. Unhybridised p orbitals overlap to form delocalised π system.
name: Hybridisation in Benzene
subject: che

text: The formula for \( \sum_{k=1}^n k(k+1) \) is:
name: Sum of k(k+1)
subject: maths

text: The first law of thermodynamics is:
options: Heat flows from hot to cold
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = Q - W \). Internal energy change = heat absorbed − work done by system.
name: First Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: The phenomenon of movement of colloidal particles under electric field is called:
explanation: Electrophoresis: movement of charged colloidal particles in an electric field. Electro-osmosis: movement of dispersion medium.
name: Electrophoresis
subject: che

text: Kirchhoff's voltage law (KVL) states:
options: Current is constant around a loop
Sum of all voltages (EMFs and drops) around any closed loop = 0
Voltage is same across all elements
explanation: KVL (loop rule): \( \sum V = \sum EMF - \sum IR = 0 \). Based on conservation of energy.
name: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law
subject: phy

text: The equation \( \frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1 \). Here \( a=4, b=3 \), so \( c = \)
explanation: \( c^2 = a^2-b^2 = 16-9 = 7 \Rightarrow c = \sqrt{7} \)
name: Foci of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: Ozone (O₃) is a better oxidising agent than O₂ because:
explanation: O₃ → O₂ + [O]. Nascent oxygen [O] is highly reactive oxidising agent. This explains O₃'s disinfecting and bleaching ability.
name: Ozone as Oxidising Agent
subject: che

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
subject: maths

text: Radioactive decay follows \( N = N_0 e^{-\lambda t} \). This is a:
explanation: Radioactive decay is a first-order process. Rate ∝ number of radioactive nuclei present.
name: Radioactive Decay Order
subject: che

explanation: \( \tan(A+B) = \frac{\tan A + \tan B}{1 - \tan A \tan B} \)
name: tan(A+B) Formula
subject: maths

text: The solution of linear first-order DE \( \frac{dy}{dx}+Py=Q \) is:
explanation: After multiplying by IF: \( \frac{d}{dx}(y\cdot\text{IF}) = Q\cdot\text{IF} \Rightarrow y\cdot\text{IF} = \int Q\cdot\text{IF}\,dx+C \)
name: Linear DE Solution
subject: maths

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
explanation: Symmetric form: \( \frac{x-x_1}{a}=\frac{y-y_1}{b}=\frac{z-z_1}{c} = \lambda \)
name: Symmetric Form of Line
subject: maths

text: The magnetic flux \(\Phi_B\) through a loop of area \(A\) in field \(B\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Magnetic flux: \( \Phi_B = \vec{B}\cdot\vec{A} = BA\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{B}\) and area normal \(\hat{n}\). Unit: Weber (Wb).
name: Magnetic Flux Formula
subject: phy

text: The equation whose roots are squares of roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Roots of original: 1, 2. Squared: 1, 4. New equation: \( (x-1)(x-4) = x^2 - 5x + 4 = 0 \)
name: Squared Roots Equation
subject: maths

text: Number of ways to form a word using all letters of EXAM is:
explanation: EXAM has 4 distinct letters. Arrangements \( = 4! = 24 \)
name: Word Formation
subject: maths

text: A fair die is rolled. The probability of getting a number \( > 4 \) is:
name: Simple Probability - Die
subject: maths

text: The elastic potential energy stored per unit volume (energy density) in a stretched wire is:
explanation: Energy density \( = \frac{1}{2}\times\text{stress}\times\text{strain} = \frac{1}{2}Y(\text{strain})^2 \). Both expressions are equivalent.
name: Elastic Energy Density
subject: phy

explanation: \( \tan^{-1}x + \cot^{-1}x = \frac{\pi}{2} \) for all real \(x\).
name: tan⁻¹ + cot⁻¹ Identity
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \ln|x|+C \). Special case of power rule when \(n=-1\).
name: Integral of 1/x
subject: maths

text: The equation \( a\sin\theta + b\cos\theta = c \) has real solutions if:
explanation: The equation has real solutions iff \( |c| \leq \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), i.e., \( c^2 \leq a^2+b^2 \).
name: Condition for Real Solutions
subject: maths

text: Enzymes are biological catalysts that are:
explanation: Enzymes are mostly globular proteins. Exception: ribozymes are catalytic RNA molecules. Highly specific, work at body temperature and neutral pH.
name: Nature of Enzymes
subject: che

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem
subject: maths
